title: schiedea diffusa subsp. diffusa
intro: Schiedea diffusa subsp. diffusa, also known as the Mauna Kea schiedeia, is a rare and endangered succulent plant native to the Hawaiian Islands. It is a member of the Caryophyllaceae family and has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as prevent the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the Mauna Kea schiedeia are often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, and are believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever and promoting wound healing.
